stories.title: Simplification of the Capital Goods Scheme — VAT changes from 29 July 2026
stories.summary: HMRC is introducing significant simplifications to the VAT Capital Goods Scheme from 29 July 2026, removing computers and certain other items from the scheme's scope to reduce compliance burdens for businesses.

stories.title: Income and Corporation Taxes (Electronic Communications) Direction Updated
stories.summary: HMRC has updated its Electronic Communications Direction for Company Tax Returns, reflecting changes to the rules governing digital filing requirements under the Income and Corporation Taxes regulations.

stories.title: UK and India New Social Security Agreement — NI Changes from 15 July 2026
stories.summary: A new UK-India social security agreement will take effect on 15 July 2026, meaning employees covered by the deal will move onto Indian social security legislation and cease paying UK Class 1 National Insurance contributions.

stories.title: Government to Change Law to Allow Deportation of Rochdale Grooming Gang Ringleader
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has announced plans to amend the 1971 Immigration Act to close a legal loophole that has prevented the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang. The legal change is intended to enable his removal from the UK.

stories.title: Mahmood to Close Loophole Blocking Deportation of Rochdale Grooming Gang Ringleader
stories.summary: The Guardian reports that the Home Secretary is expected to amend immigration law to enable the deportation of the convicted Rochdale grooming gang ringleader, who has so far been protected from removal by a gap in existing legislation.

stories.title: New UK Ambassador to the Czech Republic Appointed
stories.summary: Alastair King-Smith has been appointed as His Majesty's Ambassador to the Czech Republic, succeeding Matthew Field OBE in the diplomatic post.

stories.title: FCDO Updates Nigeria Travel Advice — Against All But Essential Travel to Kwara and Benue States
stories.summary: The FCDO has updated its Nigeria travel guidance to advise against all but essential travel to Kwara State and Benue State, citing security concerns. The FCDO continues to advise against all travel to certain other areas.

stories.title: First Comprehensive Review into PIP Finds It Is 'Not Fit for Purpose'
stories.summary: A major government-commissioned review of Personal Independence Payment (PIP) has concluded that the benefit is no longer fit for purpose and has failed to keep pace with changes in disability, health and work over the past decade. The findings are expected to drive significant welfare reform.

stories.title: Universal Credit Two-Child Limit Policy Statistics Published — April 2026
stories.summary: DWP has published new statistical data on Universal Credit claimants affected by the two-child limit policy, including updated figures on the number of families and children affected by the benefit cap.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Cheaper Vet Fees and New Ombudsman in Biggest Reforms to Vet Sector in Half a Century
stories.summary: DEFRA has announced a sweeping overhaul of the veterinary sector — the most significant in 50 years — including measures to reduce vet fees for pet owners and the creation of a new veterinary ombudsman to handle complaints. The reforms follow a Competition and Markets Authority review.

stories.title: £180 Million Overhaul Underway at RAF Brize Norton
stories.summary: A major £180 million infrastructure revamp has begun at RAF Brize Norton, the UK's busiest military air base. Works include replacing the 3km runway, refurbishing taxiways and upgrading airfield lighting to maintain the base's critical air mobility role.

stories.title: RAF Brize Norton: £180m Revamp of UK's Largest RAF Base Begins
stories.summary: BBC News reports that a £180m renovation project is underway at RAF Brize Norton, covering the main runway, taxiways and lighting systems essential to the RAF's transport and air-to-air refuelling operations.

stories.title: Defence Investment Plan — Jobs Impact Estimates Published
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has published estimates of future UK jobs supported by the Defence Investment Plan (released 30 June 2026), highlighting significant indirect employment supported in UK industry through defence spending.

stories.title: DBT Launches Skills and Capability Review of Ofgem
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade has launched a formal skills and capability review of Ofgem, the energy regulator, examining whether the regulator has the right expertise to deliver its remit in a rapidly evolving energy market.

stories.title: Industrial Strategy Quarterly Update — Economic Indicators April to June 2026
stories.summary: DBT has published its Industrial Strategy quarterly economic update covering April to June 2026, including performance indicators across the eight key industrial sectors and notable revisions to major investment data.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Digital Identity Sectoral Analysis Report 2026 Published
stories.summary: DSIT and the Office for Digital Identities and Attributes have published their 2026 research report on the UK's digital identity sector, providing an analysis of market growth, adoption trends and regulatory developments.

stories.title: 50th UK Government-Funded 4G Mast Goes Live in Rural Scotland
stories.summary: DSIT has announced that the 50th 4G mast funded by the UK Government has been switched on in rural Scotland, boosting mobile connectivity in some of the country's most hard-to-reach communities as part of the Shared Rural Network programme.

stories.title: Life Sciences Sector Plan: One Year On — Progress Report Published
stories.summary: DSIT has released a one-year-on progress report for the Life Sciences Sector Plan (launched July 2025), detailing actions taken and investments made across the life sciences industry in the UK over the past year.

stories.title: NHS England Chief Warns Staff Face Sack or Prison for Accessing Patient Records Without Cause
stories.summary: The head of NHS England has issued a stark warning to all NHS staff that accessing patient records without a legitimate clinical reason could result in dismissal or criminal prosecution, amid growing concerns about data misuse across the health service.

stories.title: 10-Year Capital Plan Boosts Access to Modern GP Services in North East and Yorkshire
stories.summary: The Government's 10-Year Capital Plan, announced today, will enable GP practices across England — including in the North East and Yorkshire — to convert unused rooms into consulting and treatment spaces, improving access to primary care services.

stories.title: Urgent Parliamentary Question on Royal Mail's Failure to Meet Universal Service Obligation
stories.summary: An urgent question was raised in the House of Commons regarding Royal Mail's ongoing failure to meet its universal service obligation, which requires delivery of letters to all UK addresses on a specified schedule. The issue has been highlighted by widespread complaints of severe postal delays.

stories.title: Residents Report Severe Postal Delays as Royal Mail Prioritises Parcels Over Letters
stories.summary: Communities including Milton Keynes have reported significant delays in letter deliveries, with residents noting that parcels are being prioritised while letter services deteriorate — despite rising stamp prices and Royal Mail's legal universal service obligations.
